subject: DIAGNOSTIC: BUG-8 — oscillator /api/oscillator hangs due to unbounded git fetch in checkNewCommits()

body.summary: The /api/oscillator endpoint hangs (curl returns 0 bytes after 5s timeout). Root cause: checkNewCommits() in oscillator.go runs `git fetch --dry-run --all` without a command timeout. When SSH to github.com stalls, the entire oscillator cycle blocks for 60-120 seconds. During this window, Snapshot() contends with cycle() for the mutex, causing the HTTP handler to hang.

evidence.endpoint_behavior: curl -v --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:8082/api/oscillator returns 0 bytes. Connection established, request sent, no response received within 5 seconds.
evidence.handler_code: oscillator.go:407 — handleOscillator calls s.Oscillator.Snapshot() which acquires o.mu.RLock(). If cycle() at line 152 holds o.mu.Lock(), RLock blocks.
evidence.blocking_function: oscillator.go checkNewCommits(): exec.Command('git', '-C', o.projectRoot, 'fetch', '--dry-run', '--all') — no context.WithTimeout. SSH timeout to github.com = 60-120 seconds of blocking.
evidence.ssh_timeout_observed: Earlier this session, deliver-to-peer.sh timed out on SSH to github.com ('ssh: connect to host github.com port 22: Operation timed out'). Same failure mode blocks the oscillator.
evidence.secondary_issue: ComputeTier at line 174 runs INSIDE the locked section and queries o.dbPath — which points to the wrong state.db (BUG-1 repo_root). If sqlite3 query on the empty DB takes time, this extends the lock hold duration.

fix.primary:
Add context.WithTimeout to the git fetch command in checkNewCommits():

ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
defer cancel()
cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, 'git', '-C', o.projectRoot, 'fetch', '--dry-run', '--all')

Caps signal check at 10 seconds regardless of network conditions.
fix.secondary: Move ComputeTier() call outside the locked section. It queries the DB (slow I/O) while holding mu.Lock — compute the tier before acquiring the lock, then write the result under lock.
fix.verification: After fix: curl http://localhost:8082/api/oscillator should return JSON within 1 second showing oscillator state (activation, threshold, signal breakdown, fire history).

oscillator_status.goroutine: Running (shadow mode) — starts on every meshd boot
oscillator_status.mode: Shadow — logs 'would fire' events but never spawns deliberations
oscillator_status.cycle_interval: Adaptive — but impaired by the git fetch blocking
oscillator_status.activation_computation: Runs against wrong state.db (inherits BUG-1 repo_root)

body.bug_inventory_update: BUG-8 added to the inventory. Complete list: BUG-1 repo_root (T6), BUG-2 dashboard unreachable (T5), BUG-3 GitHub API 403 (T7), BUG-4 /kb/dictionary 404 spam (T7), BUG-5 notification null (T7), BUG-6 KV disabled (T7), BUG-7 restart frequency (T7), BUG-8 oscillator hangs (this turn). Fix dependency: BUG-8 independent of BUG-1 — can fix in parallel.

epistemic_flags: The mutex contention diagnosis derives from code reading, not runtime lock profiling. The exact contention scenario (cycle() holds Lock while Snapshot() waits for RLock) remains the most likely explanation but not confirmed via Go pprof or mutex profiling.
The 60-120 second SSH timeout estimate comes from default SSH configuration. If the host has custom SSH timeout settings, the actual blocking duration may differ.

action_gate.gate_condition: Ops to apply context.WithTimeout fix to checkNewCommits() and move ComputeTier outside the locked section. Independent of BUG-1 fix — can ship separately.
